name: selfhost-compose-smoke
question: Does deploy/docker/docker-compose.yml parse cleanly (yaml-lint), do its application env-var references match the canonical C21 catalog in docs/operations/SELFHOST_ENV.md, and (with --full) does `docker compose down -v && up -d` from a clean state bring the stack to HTTP 200 on /health/ready within 90s?

output.schema: PASS or FAIL <n> with probe-level offender detail. Exit 0 on PASS, 1 on FAIL, 2 on environment failure.

consultation.typical_sessions_using: every-commit-during-P4+ (touching deploy/docker or SELFHOST_ENV.md); /0-uldf-finalize Phase 11 with --full

consumer_scope: P4+ deploy/docker/** + docs/operations/SELFHOST_ENV.md (every commit touching either; CI gate post-launch with --full)
fallback: Probe A: `docker compose -f deploy/docker/docker-compose.yml config --quiet` should exit 0. Probe B: extract `${FEEDBACKMONK_*}`, `${DATABASE_URL}` from compose; confirm each name appears as a backticked first-cell entry in a markdown table row of docs/operations/SELFHOST_ENV.md (above the `## Out of Scope` section). Probe C: `docker compose -f deploy/docker/docker-compose.yml down -v && docker compose -f deploy/docker/docker-compose.yml up -d --build --wait && curl -fsS http://localhost:${FEEDBACKMONK_PORT:-14304}/health/ready` should print `{"status":"ok","db_connected":true,...}` with HTTP 200.

provenance.authored_by: P4 Stage 2 Worker B — CLAUDE-B (Task Zero — collab-20260514-170323)
provenance.rationale: FR-FBR-17 self-host distribution + DEC-FBR-IMPL-06 three-probe oracle decision + Contracts C21 (env catalog SSOT) and C24 (three-probe schema). Three-leg defense per P4 plan § Testability Gate: (1) env-reader chokepoints in crates/feedbackmonk-api/src/main.rs (Tier-1 fail-fast); (2) THIS oracle (yaml-lint + env-doc-xref + clean-state smoke — Tier-2 mechanical); (3) operator runbook docs/operations/SELFHOST.md (Tier-3 cold-readability). Cold-start vacuous-PASS supports Task Zero order-of-operations — oracle authored BEFORE the compose stack, then re-evaluated as Phase 1 lands.
